sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच।
एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते।
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
evaṃ satata-yuktā ye bhaktās tvāṃ paryupāsate
ye cāpy akṣaram avyaktaṃ teṣāṃ ke yoga-vittamāḥ
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: 'Those devotees who, ever-integrated, thus worship You, and those [who worship] the imperishable unmanifest—among them, who are the top knowers of yoga?'
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks which path—personal devotion to Krishna or impersonal meditation on the formless Absolute—yields the deepest, most secure realization. He seeks validation for his own relational choice while acknowledging an alternative route.
psychological_state: Healthy comparative anxiety—Arjuna checks whether his emotionally intimate bond with Krishna is as spiritually legitimate as the abstract, impersonal route favored by many sages.
trigger_scenario: A therapy client who has committed to a warm, relational mindfulness practice asks, 'Am I missing something by not doing the more austere, object-less meditation my friends swear by?'
anchor_text: Choose the path that lets your heart feel closest to the Divine.
